ul.li: Environment: Frequency scaling disabled; CPU pinned to Core 0.
Validity: CV < 2% across 15 iterations per kernel.
Evidence: Speedup claims must map to specific SIMD instructions identified in annotation.
ISA: Native architecture (-march=native) overrides generic SIMD.

g.title: Vectorization-friendly scalar code with C99 'restrict' keywords
Hand-written SSE/AVX implementations from M1-M3
Rigorous benchmark harness with RDTSC/POSIX timing statistics
Loop vectorized / not vectorized with dependency analysis
objdump -d output including XMM/YMM register usage
ELF-64 binary with pinned affinity
Scans for instructions: [V]MULPS, [V]ADDPS, [V]HADDPS, [V]SHUFPS
Instruction count per type: [load] [store] [compute] [shuffle]
CPU model, frequency pinning (cpupower), hyperthreading status
Populate L1/L2 caches, stabilize frequency scaling
High-precision timing via clock_gettime(CLOCK_MONOTONIC_RAW)
Critical: Coefficient of Variation (CV) < 2% threshold
Relative performance factor Calculation
Compiles Markdown including LaTeX for speedup formulas
Executive summary, hardware platform, vectorization evidence, benchmark logs
